violations.text: Raw animal foods such as ground beef were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods such as tomatoes in the walk-in refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/28/2025

The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by droppings observed during time of inspection (in storage area and near mop sink). The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. New Violation. Correct By: 10/28/2025

Pipe (unused) in basement observed leaking during time of inspection. Plumbing systems to be in good repair and proper adjustment. No storage in basement for establishment per owner. New Violation. Correct By: 10/09/2025
Partition required between mop sink and food prep sink; and handsink and food prep sink. New Violation. Correct By: 10/07/2025
The food prep sink is lacking an air gap. New Violation. Correct By: 10/07/2025
Dumpster lid was observed in poor repair; tight fitting lids are required for disposal units kept outside. New Violation. Correct By: 10/07/2025
The stairwell leading to the basement have an accumulation of old droppings. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 09/30/2025

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the basement area. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. New Violation. Correct By: 10/07/2025
The ceiling in the kitchen is covered with tiles that are not smooth, durable, and easily cleanable. New Violation. Correct By: 10/07/2025
